Let $J$ be the set of all sequences of real numbers, and let $A$, $L$ and $P$
be three mappings from $J$ to $J$ defined as follows. If $x = \{x_n\} = \{x_0,
x_1, x_2, \dots \} \in J$, then

$$
\begin{align*}
  Ax = \lbrace x_n + 1 \rbrace & = \lbrace x_0 + 1, x_1 + 1, x_2 + 1, \dots \rbrace, \newline
                Lx & = \lbrace 1, x_0, x_1, x_2, \dots \rbrace, \newline
                Px & = \left\lbrace \sum_{k=0}^{n} x_k \right\rbrace.
\end{align*}
$$
Finally, define the composite mapping $T$ on $J$ by $T x = L \circ A \circ P
x$. In the following, let $y = \lbrace 1, 1, 1, \dots\rbrace$.

\begin{enumerate}[(a)]

\item Write down $T^2 y$, giving the first eight terms of the sequence and a
      closed formula for the $n$-th term.
\item Assuming that $z = \lbrace z_n \rbrace = \displaystyle{\lim_{i \to
      \infty} T^i y}$ exists, conjecture the general form for $z_n$, and prove
      your conjecture.

\end{enumerate}
